lede: A look at April 16 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Sadie Sink and Marié Digby, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Børsen
events.desc: The historic Børsen in Copenhagen, Denmark, is severely damaged by a fire.

events.title: Virginia Tech shooting
events.desc: Virginia Tech shooting: Seung-Hui Cho murders 32 people and injures 17 before committing suicide.

events.title: OECD
events.desc: The Organization of European Economic Co-operation is formed.

events.title: Bernard Baruch
events.desc: Bernard Baruch first applies the term "Cold War" to describe the relationship between the United States and the Soviet Union.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Italian-German Tarigo convoy is attacked and destroyed by British ships.

events.title: Mahatma Gandhi
events.desc: Mohandas Gandhi organizes a day of "prayer and fasting" in response to the killing of Indian protesters in the Jallianwala Bagh massacre by the British colonial troops three days earlier.

events.title: Harriet Quimby
events.desc: Harriet Quimby becomes the first woman to fly an airplane across the English Channel.

events.title: French Revolutionary Wars
events.desc: French Revolutionary Wars: The Battle of Mount Tabor: Napoleon drives Ottoman Turks across the River Jordan near Acre.

events.title: Revolt of the Comuneros
events.desc: The Revolt of the Comuneros begins in Spain against the rule of Charles V.

events.title: AD 69
events.desc: Defeated by Vitellius' troops at Bedriacum, Roman emperor Otho commits suicide.

deaths.text: Justin Fairfax, American lawyer and politician, 41st Lieutenant Governor of Virginia
George Kunda, Zambian lawyer and politician, 11th Vice-President of Zambia

observances.text: Christian feast day: Isabella Gilmore (Church of England)
Christian feast day: Molly Brant (Konwatsijayenni) (Anglican Church of Canada, Episcopal Church)
